plan: Create a shared React surface module for the preview families so Storybook and the example app render the same compositions.
Add a dedicated Storybook story file that exposes one preview per mockup family and surfaces meaningful args/controls rather than static snapshots.
Refactor the basic usage example app into a navigable mockup gallery that reuses the shared surface module instead of maintaining a separate one-off landing page.
Keep package API stable unless a small internal export is the cleanest way to reuse the shared mockup module across local source and the example app.
Verify the work with deterministic gates: mockup story coverage grep, typecheck, library build, Storybook build, and example build.
runtimeCallPaths: project/preview/*.html -> visual and structural reference only; do not edit these files
verificationNotes: Every preview family in project/preview should appear by name in the shared source module and Storybook story file.
The example app should reference the shared mockup surfaces rather than only component demos.
No unrelated component APIs or token contracts should change unless required for reuse or fidelity.

instructions: $babysitter:call resume work on this repo, improve, fill the gaps, tech debts, pixel pushing, until it perfectly fits the mocks in project and have storybook previews for each mockup and their controls, etc. and in the examples.
Implement React mockup surfaces that match the project preview sources as closely as practical with the existing compendium tokens, technical CSS, components, and icons.
Create or update Storybook stories so each project preview mock surface has a preview and meaningful controls.
Reuse the same shared React surfaces in the basic usage example app so the examples cover the same mock families.
Prefer shared source files over duplicating markup across Storybook and the example app.
Keep package public API stable unless exporting a shared internal surface is clearly required for reuse.
Do not touch unrelated files or add speculative abstractions.
prompt.task: Implement the requested brownfield remediation completely, not as a proposal.

title: Implement shared mockup surfaces, stories, and example-app parity

Keep package API stable unless a small internal export is the cleanest way to reuse the shared mockup module across local source and the example app.
Verify the work with deterministic gates: mockup story coverage grep, typecheck, library build, Storybook build, and example build.
runtimeCallPaths: project/preview/*.html -> visual and structural reference only; do not edit these files
verificationNotes: Every preview family in project/preview should appear by name in the shared source module and Storybook story file.
The example app should reference the shared mockup surfaces rather than only component demos.
No unrelated component APIs or token contracts should change unless required for reuse or fidelity.
